list.name: Content ownership
description: As you know (bottom right corner), Claude (AI) was used to build these pages.
All pictures and all text are created by Joy and I.
Claude is used solely as the wordpress expert, helping organize the structure of the pages.

list.name: Accuracy disclaimer
description: When going fishing, my father (or so he claimed) would bring a fishing book to throw in the lake prior to starting to fish, so the fish would know how to behave. Sadly, I fear he was mistaken.
We are human (i.e. we make mistakes) and live in a dynamic world.
These and other factors combine to ensure what you read today feels inaccurate.
We do our best, but, let's face it: That trail was awesome last year, but is dangerous this year due to a land slide over the winter.
Point is, there will be inaccuracies - accept it and validate if you need a guaranteed source.

list.name: Work in progress
description: This site grows as we explore.
Many destination pages are placeholders waiting for a visit.
If a page looks sparse, it just means we haven't been there yet — or haven't found the words for it.

list.name: Photography
description: All photography is original, unaltered and free to use.
Some are taken by me, the beautiful ones by Joy.
Virtually all of them are available as hires, just click on the picture to see the full size.
Unfortunately, while the originals are geo-tagged, wordpress removes that information.

list.name: Why do I get confused
description: There are lakes.
Near lakes (and other places) are small rustic recreational sites intended for hiking, camping, or other outdoor activities.
Also, there are parks, intended more for nature conservation than recreational use.
Campground of all sorts are peppered among recreational sites, parks as well as several private ones.
There are also a few privately maintain sites, here classified as recreational sites, such as the day use area at Echo lake.
